notes: Reproduced memory leak in ToHarfBuzzBlob() else branch. When SKStreamAsset.GetMemoryBase() returns IntPtr.Zero (file-backed streams, Android fonts), the blob's release callback only frees the unmanaged copy but never disposes the original SKStreamAsset. Demonstrated via SKFileStream where 1000 create/dispose cycles leaked ~6MB, while the if branch (memory-mapped streams) showed 0 KB delta. Bug exists on 3.116.0, 3.119.2, and main branch. Two open PRs (#3376 and #3466) address this but neither has been merged.

reproductionSteps.description: Create console project with SkiaSharp 3.116.0 and SkiaSharp.HarfBuzz 3.116.0
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 3.116.0 && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.HarfBuzz --version 3.116.0

filesCreated.description: Repro code that tests both branches of ToHarfBuzzBlob() by using SKFileStream (else branch) and typeface.OpenStream (if branch), measuring working set delta over 1000 iterations

reproductionSteps.description: Build and run repro — SKFileStream triggers the else branch (GetMemoryBase() == Zero), showing ~6MB leak over 1000 iterations vs 0 KB for the if branch
reproductionSteps.output:
GetMemoryBase: Zero (else/LEAK)
Else branch (SKFileStream) peak delta: 6064 KB
If branch (OpenStream) peak delta: 0 KB
LEAK DETECTED: Else branch leaked ~6064 KB more than if branch

reproductionSteps.description: Test with latest stable SkiaSharp 3.119.2 in a fresh project directory — same leak pattern persists
reproductionSteps.output:
GetMemoryBase: Zero (else/LEAK)
Else branch (SKFileStream) peak delta: 6384 KB
If branch (OpenStream) peak delta: 0 KB
LEAK DETECTED: Else branch leaked ~6384 KB more than if branch

reproductionSteps.description: Verify main branch source still has the bug — BlobExtensions.cs else branch (line 30) only frees unmanaged copy, never disposes SKStreamAsset. PRs #3376 and #3466 open but not merged.
reproductionSteps.output:
Line 24 (if branch): blob = new Blob(memoryBase, size, ReadOnly, () => asset.Dispose()); // OK
Line 30 (else branch): blob = new Blob(ptr, size, ReadOnly, () => Marshal.FreeCoTaskMem(ptr)); // LEAK - asset not disposed

versionResults.notes: Else branch leaks ~6MB over 1000 iterations via SKFileStream. If branch shows 0 KB delta.
Same leak pattern as 3.116.0 — ~6.4MB over 1000 iterations in fresh project dir.
Source inspection confirms BlobExtensions.cs still has inconsistent disposal. PRs #3376 and #3466 open but not merged.

corrections.corrected: backend/Metal is incorrect — this is a managed code bug in SkiaSharp.HarfBuzz BlobExtensions.cs, not related to Metal rendering backend

actionability.reason: Bug reproduced on all versions including main. Two open PRs exist (#3376, #3466) but neither merged. Maintainer prefers #3466 approach (caller manages stream lifecycle).

actions.description: Fix area label to SkiaSharp.HarfBuzz, remove incorrect backend/Metal

actions.comment:
We've confirmed this memory leak. The root cause is in `BlobExtensions.ToHarfBuzzBlob()` — the `else` branch (triggered when `SKStreamAsset.GetMemoryBase()` returns `IntPtr.Zero`) copies data to unmanaged memory but never disposes the original `SKStreamAsset`.

**Reproduction evidence:**

Using `SKFileStream` (which has `GetMemoryBase() == IntPtr.Zero`), 1000 create/dispose cycles leaked ~6MB of native memory. The `if` branch (memory-mapped streams) showed 0 KB delta — confirming the inconsistency.

**Tested versions:**
| Version | Result |
|---------|--------|
| 3.116.0 (reporter) | ❌ Reproduced (~6MB leak / 1000 iterations) |
| 3.119.2 (latest) | ❌ Reproduced (~6.4MB leak / 1000 iterations) |
| main (source) | ❌ Bug present in source |

**Workaround:** Reuse `SKShaper` instances per typeface rather than creating new ones frequently.

PRs #3376 and #3466 both address this — #3466 takes the maintainer-preferred approach of having the caller (`SKShaper`) manage stream disposal rather than the blob extension method.

workarounds: Reuse SKShaper instances per typeface instead of creating/disposing frequently
Manually separate OpenStream() and ToHarfBuzzBlob() calls to control stream disposal

proposedResponse.body:
We've confirmed this memory leak. The root cause is in `BlobExtensions.ToHarfBuzzBlob()` — the `else` branch (triggered when `SKStreamAsset.GetMemoryBase()` returns `IntPtr.Zero`) copies data to unmanaged memory but never disposes the original `SKStreamAsset`.

**Reproduction evidence:**

Using `SKFileStream` (which has `GetMemoryBase() == IntPtr.Zero`), 1000 create/dispose cycles leaked ~6MB of native memory. The `if` branch (memory-mapped streams) showed 0 KB delta — confirming the inconsistency.

**Tested versions:**
| Version | Result |
|---------|--------|
| 3.116.0 (reporter) | ❌ Reproduced (~6MB leak / 1000 iterations) |
| 3.119.2 (latest) | ❌ Reproduced (~6.4MB leak / 1000 iterations) |
| main (source) | ❌ Bug present in source |

**Workaround:** Reuse `SKShaper` instances per typeface rather than creating new ones frequently.

PRs #3376 and #3466 both address this — #3466 takes the maintainer-preferred approach of having the caller (`SKShaper`) manage stream disposal rather than the blob extension method.
